questions.question: Define Queue as an Abstract Data Type (ADT). Explain the fundamental principle it follows.
keyPoints: Queue is a linear ADT
Follows FIFO (First-In-First-Out) principle
Elements are added at rear and removed from front
Operations: enqueue, dequeue, front, rear, isEmpty, isFull

questions.question: Explain the difference between linear queue and circular queue with a suitable example.
keyPoints: Linear queue wastes space after dequeue operations
Circular queue wraps rear to front when it reaches end
Circular queue efficiently reuses deleted spaces
Example: In linear queue with size 5, after 5 dequeues, cannot enqueue even if space is available

questions.question: What is overflow and underflow conditions in a queue? How are these handled?
keyPoints: Overflow: Attempting to insert in full queue
Underflow: Attempting to delete from empty queue
Handled by checking isFull() before enqueue
Handled by checking isEmpty() before dequeue
Appropriate error messages or exceptions are thrown

questions.question: Describe the queue implementation using linked list. What are its advantages over array implementation?
keyPoints: Linked list nodes contain data and next pointer
Enqueue adds at tail, dequeue removes from head
Dynamic size - no fixed capacity limitation
No memory wastage - grows and shrinks with elements
Memory allocated as needed

questions.question: Write pseudocode for enqueue and dequeue operations in a queue.
keyPoints: Enqueue: Check overflow, increment rear, add element at rear
Dequeue: Check underflow, get element at front, increment front
Return the removed element
Reset queue if empty after dequeue

questions.question: What is a priority queue? How does it differ from a simple queue?
keyPoints: Priority queue serves elements based on priority, not FIFO
Higher priority elements are dequeued first
Can be ascending or descending order
Simple queue strictly follows FIFO
Applications: CPU scheduling, Dijkstra's algorithm

questions.question: Explain the BFS algorithm and the role of queue in it.
keyPoints: BFS traverses graph level by level
Starts from source vertex, marks it visited
Uses queue to store vertices to be explored
Explores all neighbors of front vertex before moving to next level
Ensures shortest path in unweighted graphs

questions.question: Explain the array implementation of a circular queue. Write pseudocode for enqueue and dequeue operations. Also discuss how empty and full conditions are differentiated.
keyPoints: Use modulo operator to wrap rear and front pointers
Initialize front = rear = 0
Enqueue: check if (rear + 1) % MAXSIZE == front (full)
Dequeue: check if front == rear (empty)
After enqueue: rear = (rear + 1) % MAXSIZE
After dequeue: front = (front + 1) % MAXSIZE
Store at rear index, retrieve from front index
Can store MAXSIZE-1 elements to distinguish empty/full

questions.question: Write a C program to implement queue using array with all necessary operations: enqueue, dequeue, display, isEmpty, isFull.
keyPoints: Define MAXSIZE and queue array
Initialize front and rear variables
isFull: rear == MAXSIZE - 1
isEmpty: front == -1 or front > rear
enqueue: check overflow, increment rear, add element
dequeue: check underflow, get front element, increment front
display: iterate from front to rear
Handle all boundary conditions properly

questions.question: What is a Deque (Double-Ended Queue)? Explain its types and applications.
keyPoints: Deque allows insertion and deletion at both ends
Types: Input-restricted (can insert only at rear, delete at both)
Types: Output-restricted (can insert at both, delete only at front)
Applications: Palindrome checker,Undo-Redo operations
Used in sliding window problems
Can be implemented using array or linked list

questions.question: Discuss various applications of queue data structure in computer science.
keyPoints: CPU/Process scheduling in operating systems
Disk scheduling algorithms
Breadth-First Search (BFS) in graphs
Level-order traversal of trees
Print job spooling in printers
Customer service systems
Message queues in networking
Handling asynchronous data transfer (I/O buffers)
